[[ja:白猪屯倉]]
<!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--> [[Category:Imperial Storehouses]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q55526455}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
{{wikidata link|Q55526455}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q55526455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:白猪屯倉遺阯碑.JPG|thumb|280px|right|Monument marking the site of Shirai no Miyake (Gotan, Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)]] '''Shirai no Miyake''' (白猪屯倉) was an imperial estate (屯倉) established in [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]] under the direct control of the [[Yamato Court]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the 19th volume, it was established in [[:en:555 AD|555 AD]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. The court dispatched [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]], the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>
By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>
In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>
== Historical Verification == There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake: 1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) 2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province 3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}
The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>
Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.
== Regional and Political Context == Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by [[:en:Northern Wei|Northern Wei]]'s [[:en:Equal-field system|Equal-field system]], transmitted via [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]].<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q55526455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:白猪屯倉遺阯碑.JPG|thumb|280px|right|Monument marking the site of Shirai no Miyake (Gotan, Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)]] '''Shirai no Miyake''' (白猪屯倉) was an imperial estate (屯倉) established in [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]] under the direct control of the [[Yamato Court]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the 19th volume, it was established in [[:en:555 AD|555 AD]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. The court dispatched [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]], the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>
By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>
In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>
== Historical Verification == There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake: 1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) 2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province 3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}
The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>
Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.
== Regional and Political Context == Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by [[:en:Northern Wei|Northern Wei]]'s [[:en:Equal-field system|Equal-field system]], transmitted via [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]].<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volumes 3 and 4, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 3),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1988 * ''Shoku Nihongi: Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 2),'' Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 1992 * Inoue Mitsusada, ''The Transition from Myth to History,'' [[:en:Chūōkōronsha|Chūōkōronsha]], 1965 * Inoue Mitsusada, ''The Emperor and Ancient Power,'' [[Iwanami Gendai Bunko]], 2000 * Kishi Toshio (Ed.), ''Conflict over Ancient Japanese Power,'' [[Chūkō Bunko]], 1996
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] * [[:en:Kibi Clan Rebellion|Revolt of the Kibi clan]] * {{ill|Miyake (ancient Japan)|ja|屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake (ancient Japan)|1=Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465761}} * [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] * [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] * [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] * [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] * [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] * [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Revolt of Iwai]] * {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}} * {{ill|Kibi no Kaefu Nanba|ja|吉備海部難波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Kaefu Nanba|1=Kibi no Ama no Naniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526276}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q55526455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:白猪屯倉遺阯碑.JPG|thumb|280px|right|Monument marking the site of Shirai no Miyake (Gotan, Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)]] '''Shirai no Miyake''' (白猪屯倉) was an imperial estate (屯倉) established in [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]] under the direct control of the [[Yamato Court]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the 19th volume, it was established in [[:en:555 AD|555 AD]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. The court dispatched [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]], the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>
By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>
In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>
== Historical Verification == There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake: 1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) 2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province 3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}
The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>
Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.
== Regional and Political Context == Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by [[:en:Northern Wei|Northern Wei]]'s [[:en:Equal-field system|Equal-field system]], transmitted via [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]].<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>

<!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--><!--Shirai no Miyake--> [[Category:Imperial Storehouses]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q55526455}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q55526455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:白猪屯倉遺阯碑.JPG|thumb|280px|right|Monument marking the site of Shirai no Miyake (Gotan, Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)]] '''Shirai no Miyake''' (白猪屯倉) was an imperial estate (屯倉) established in [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]] under the direct control of the [[Yamato Court]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the 19th volume, it was established in [[:en:555 AD|555 AD]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. The court dispatched [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]], the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>
By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>
In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>
== Historical Verification == There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake: 1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) 2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province 3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}
The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>
Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.
== Regional and Political Context == Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by [[:en:Northern Wei|Northern Wei]]'s [[:en:Equal-field system|Equal-field system]], transmitted via [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]].<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * ''Nihon Shoki,'' Volumes 3 and 4, [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995 * ''Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 3),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], 1988 * ''Shoku Nihongi: Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 2),'' Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 1992 * Inoue Mitsusada, ''The Transition from Myth to History,'' [[:en:Chūōkōronsha|Chūōkōronsha]], 1965 * Inoue Mitsusada, ''The Emperor and Ancient Power,'' [[Iwanami Gendai Bunko]], 2000 * Kishi Toshio (Ed.), ''Conflict over Ancient Japanese Power,'' [[Chūkō Bunko]], 1996
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Kibi clan|Kibi clan]] * [[:en:Kibi Clan Rebellion|Revolt of the Kibi clan]] * {{ill|Miyake (ancient Japan)|ja|屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake (ancient Japan)|1=Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465761}} * [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] * [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]] * [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] * [[:en:Emperor Bidatsu|Emperor Bidatsu]] * [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] * [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] * [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Revolt of Iwai]] * {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}} * {{ill|Kibi no Kaefu Nanba|ja|吉備海部難波|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Kaefu Nanba|1=Kibi no Ama no Naniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526276}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白猪屯倉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55526455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:白猪屯倉遺阯碑.JPG|thumb|280px|right|Monument marking the site of Shirai no Miyake (Gotan, Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)]] '''Shirai no Miyake''' (白猪屯倉) was an imperial estate (屯倉) established in [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]] under the direct control of the [[Yamato Court]].
== Overview == According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], in the 19th volume, it was established in [[:en:555 AD|555 AD]] during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. The court dispatched [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]], the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>
By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>
In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>
== Historical Verification == There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake: 1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) 2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province 3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}
The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>
Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.
== Regional and Political Context == Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by [[:en:Northern Wei|Northern Wei]]'s [[:en:Equal-field system|Equal-field system]], transmitted via [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]].<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>

As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>
In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>
== Historical Verification == There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake: 1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture) 2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province 3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}
The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>
Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.
== Regional and Political Context == Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] and [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]]. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by [[:en:Northern Wei|Northern Wei]]'s [[:en:Equal-field system|Equal-field system]], transmitted via [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]].<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>

'''Shirai no Miyake''' (白猪屯倉) was an imperial estate (屯倉) established in {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|WD=Q1155128|lt=Kibi Province}} under the direct control of the [[Yamato Court]].

== Overview ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, in the 19th volume, it was established in [[:en:555 AD|555 AD]] during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}. The court dispatched {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname}}, the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>

By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>

In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}} increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>

== Historical Verification ==
There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake:
1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)
2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province
3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}

The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>

Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}, and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.

== Regional and Political Context ==
Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}} and {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by {{ill|Northern Wei|en|Northern Wei|WD=Q875305|lt=Northern Wei}}'s {{ill|Equal-field system|en|Equal-field system|WD=Q710066|lt=Equal-field system}}, transmitted via {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:白猪屯倉遺阯碑.JPG|thumb|280px|right|Monument marking the site of Shirai no Miyake (Gotan, Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)]]
'''Shirai no Miyake''' (白猪屯倉) was an imperial estate (屯倉) established in {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|WD=Q1155128|lt=Kibi Province}} under the direct control of the [[Yamato Court]].

== Overview ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, in the 19th volume, it was established in [[:en:555 AD|555 AD]] during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}. The court dispatched {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname}}, the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>

By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>

In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}} increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>

== Historical Verification ==
There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake:
1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)
2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province
3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}

The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>

Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}, and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.

== Regional and Political Context ==
Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}} and {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by {{ill|Northern Wei|en|Northern Wei|WD=Q875305|lt=Northern Wei}}'s {{ill|Equal-field system|en|Equal-field system|WD=Q710066|lt=Equal-field system}}, transmitted via {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>

The court dispatched {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname}}, the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>

By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Shirai no Itsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>

In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako}} increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>

== Historical Verification ==
There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake:
1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)
2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province
3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Kojima no Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}

The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>

Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}, and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.

== Regional and Political Context ==
Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku}} and {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by {{ill|Northern Wei|en|Northern Wei|WD=Q875305|lt=Northern Wei}}'s {{ill|Equal-field system|en|Equal-field system|WD=Q710066|lt=Equal-field system}}, transmitted via {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>

[[File:白猪屯倉遺阯碑.JPG|thumb|280px|right|Monument marking the site of Shirai no Miyake (Gotan, Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)]]
'''Shirai no Miyake''' (白猪屯倉) was an imperial estate (屯倉) established in {{ill|Kibi Province|en|Kibi Province|WD=Q1155128|lt=Kibi Province|1=Kingdom of Kibi}} under the direct control of the [[Yamato Court]].

== Overview ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, in the 19th volume, it was established in [[:en:555 AD|555 AD]] during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}. The court dispatched {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname|1=Soga no Iname}}, the Ōomi (Great Minister), and Hozumi no Iwayumi Omi to set up the estate in five districts of Kibi Province.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇16年7月4日条</ref>

By [[:en:569 AD|569 AD]], about ten years after the establishment of the {{ill|Tabe|ja|田部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tabe|1=Tabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24886872}} (administrative farming groups), many individuals of age were avoiding registration in the family registers and evading taxation. As a result, {{ill|Oujin no Toki|ja|王辰爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oujin no Toki|1=Shinni Ou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15113838}}'s nephew, {{ill|Shirai no Itsu|ja|白猪胆津|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Itsu|1=Q55526461|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526461}}, was sent to oversee the Shirai Tabe. He conducted inspections and implemented family registrations for taxation purposes, creating official registers known as "田戸" (Tabe households). For his contributions, Shirai no Itsu was granted the Shirai surname and the kabane (hereditary title) of {{ill|Fuhito|ja|史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuhito|1=史|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412098}} (scribe).<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇30th year 1月1日条</ref>

In the reign of [[Emperor Bidatsu]], around [[:en:574 AD|574 AD]], the Ōomi {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako|1=Soga no Umako}} increased the scope of Shirai no Miyake and its Tabe workers, granting the official family registers of Shirai to Shirai no Itsu.<ref>『日本書紀』敏達天皇3rd year 10月9日条</ref>

== Historical Verification ==
There are several theories about the location of Shirai no Miyake:
1. Ōba District (modern-day Maniwa City, Okayama Prefecture)
2. Mountainous areas in northern Kibi Province
3. Overlap with {{ill|Kojima no Miyake|ja|児島屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima no Miyake|1=Q55526459|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526459}}

The most prominent theory links it to the Gotan temple ruins in Maniwa City, formerly part of the ancient Ōba District. This is supported by records in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, which mention figures such as Shirai no Omi Ōtari and Shirai no Omi Akito, associated with Ōba District.<ref>『続日本紀』天平神護2nd year 12月29日条、神護景雲2nd year 5月3日条</ref>

Artifacts from the Gotan temple site, such as roof tiles resembling those of {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}, and the location's proximity to the strategic [[Izumo Kaidō]], reinforce the connection between Shirai no Miyake and ancient Yamato governance.

== Regional and Political Context ==
Shirai no Miyake, along with Kojima no Miyake, was strategically placed to isolate and weaken the power of the Kibi ruling authority, a measure intensified during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} and {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}. It reflects the Yamato court's evolving governance practices, possibly influenced by {{ill|Northern Wei|en|Northern Wei|WD=Q875305|lt=Northern Wei|1=Northern Wei}}'s {{ill|Equal-field system|en|Equal-field system|WD=Q710066|lt=Equal-field system|1=Equal-field system}}, transmitted via {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}.<ref>『日本書紀』欽明天皇31st year 4月2日条</ref>
